title: Bootstrap baseline — naive TinyLocNet direct-coordinate regressor
hypothesis: A ~300k-param from-scratch CNN trained for 3 epochs will produce a valid end-to-end harness run but nowhere near the 20 m target; its error should approach the trivial 'predict the area center' bound (~2.9 km for a 7 km box), confirming the metric, gates, and lineage plumbing are trustworthy before the autoresearch loop starts.
method: Frozen pipeline run unmodified on two structurally different bboxes (berlin dense-urban, prignitz rural): Sentinel-2 fetch -> 6-bucket relighting -> per-area TinyLocNet training with rotation augmentation -> ONNX export -> frozen §6 scoring with deployment gates.
expected_outcome: Primary worst-case median error in the 2-3 km range; all deployment gates pass (ONNX ~0.4 MiB << 4 MiB, host latency << 250 ms); coverage degrades in dark rural buckets.
